title: Renting a Bike in Copenhagen
summary: An Australian tourist visits a bike rental shop in Copenhagen and asks about prices, helmets, locks, and the best route along the harbour.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Good morning. I'd like to rent a bike for the day. Is that possible?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Of course. Just one day, or longer? We have hourly, daily, and weekly rates.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Just today, please. I want to ride along the harbour and maybe up to the little mermaid statue.

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: That's a lovely route. A standard city bike is one hundred and twenty crowns for the day. An electric one is two hundred and fifty.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: I'll take the standard one. I haven't ridden much lately, but the city is quite flat, right?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Very flat. You shouldn't have any trouble. Could I see some ID, please? And we'll need a small deposit.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Here's my passport. How much is the deposit?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Two hundred crowns. You'll get it back when you return the bike before six o'clock.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Got it. Do you provide a helmet?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Yes, helmets are free with every rental. It's not the law here, but we always recommend one.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: I'll definitely wear one. And what about a lock?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: Every bike comes with a wheel lock. If you stop somewhere busy, please also use the cable lock — it's in the small bag on the back.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Sounds good. One last thing — do you have a map with a quiet route to the harbour?

body.speaker: Clerk
body.text: I'll mark one for you. Stay on the green cycle paths, and you'll be at the water in fifteen minutes. Enjoy your ride!

annotations.exampleEn: You can rent a bike near the train station.

annotations.meaningVi: giá theo giờ, ngày và tuần
annotations.exampleEn: The hotel offers hourly, daily, and weekly rates.
We walked along the harbour at sunset.

annotations.meaningVi: bức tượng Nàng tiên cá nhỏ (ở Copenhagen)
annotations.exampleEn: Tourists take photos of the little mermaid statue every day.

annotations.meaningVi: xe đạp thành phố thông thường
annotations.exampleEn: A standard city bike is enough for short trips.
The town is quite flat and easy to walk.
Please pay a small deposit at the desk.
Always close the wheel lock before you leave the bike.
Use the cable lock for extra safety in the city.
Copenhagen has wonderful cycle paths everywhere.
Here are the keys — enjoy your ride!

grammarNotes.title: 'will' and contractions for offers and predictions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will' (hoặc dạng rút gọn ''ll') để đưa ra lời đề nghị hoặc dự đoán ngay tại thời điểm nói. Trong bài: 'I'll take the standard one.' (quyết định ngay) và 'I'll mark one for you.' (lời đề nghị giúp đỡ).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will' (often contracted to ''ll') for on-the-spot offers and decisions. From the dialogue: 'I'll take the standard one' and 'I'll mark one for you' are decisions made at the moment of speaking.

grammarNotes.title: Modals 'should' and 'shouldn't' for advice
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'should + V' đưa ra lời khuyên tích cực; 'shouldn't + V' đưa ra cảnh báo nhẹ. Trong bài: 'You shouldn't have any trouble' nghĩa là người nói tin rằng người nghe sẽ không gặp khó khăn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'should + verb' to give advice and 'shouldn't + verb' to warn or reassure. From the dialogue: 'You shouldn't have any trouble' — the clerk reassures the tourist that the ride will be easy.

translationVi:
Tourist: Chào buổi sáng. Tôi muốn thuê xe đạp một ngày. Có được không ạ?
Clerk: Tất nhiên. Chỉ một ngày, hay lâu hơn? Chúng tôi có giá theo giờ, ngày và tuần.
Tourist: Chỉ hôm nay thôi. Tôi muốn đạp dọc bến cảng và có thể lên tới tượng Nàng tiên cá nhỏ.
Clerk: Tuyến đường đẹp đó. Một chiếc xe đạp thành phố tiêu chuẩn là một trăm hai mươi crown cả ngày. Xe điện là hai trăm năm mươi.
Tourist: Vậy tôi lấy chiếc tiêu chuẩn. Tôi cũng không đạp nhiều dạo này, nhưng thành phố khá bằng phẳng phải không?
Clerk: Rất bằng phẳng. Anh sẽ không gặp khó khăn gì đâu. Anh cho tôi xem giấy tờ tuỳ thân được không? Và chúng tôi cần một khoản đặt cọc nhỏ.
Tourist: Đây là hộ chiếu của tôi. Đặt cọc bao nhiêu vậy?
Clerk: Hai trăm crown. Anh sẽ được hoàn lại khi trả xe trước sáu giờ chiều.
Tourist: Rõ rồi. Có mũ bảo hiểm không?
Clerk: Có, mũ bảo hiểm miễn phí kèm theo mỗi xe. Ở đây không bắt buộc, nhưng chúng tôi luôn khuyên đội.
Tourist: Tôi chắc chắn sẽ đội. Còn ổ khoá thì sao?
Clerk: Mỗi xe đều có khoá bánh. Nếu anh dừng ở nơi đông người, hãy dùng thêm khoá dây cáp — nó nằm trong cái túi nhỏ phía sau.
Tourist: Tốt rồi. Còn một điều nữa — có bản đồ với tuyến đường ít xe đến cảng không?
Clerk: Tôi sẽ đánh dấu cho anh. Cứ đi theo các đường xe đạp màu xanh, mười lăm phút là anh ra tới biển. Chúc anh đi vui vẻ!

questions.prompt: How long does the tourist want to rent the bike?
questions.explanation: He says 'I'd like to rent a bike for the day' and confirms 'Just today, please.'
questions.hint: Listen for the early confirmation about time.

questions.prompt: Where does he plan to ride?
options: Along the harbour and to the little mermaid statue
Across the bridge to another city
questions.explanation: 'I want to ride along the harbour and maybe up to the little mermaid statue.'

questions.prompt: How much does a standard city bike cost for the day?
questions.explanation: 'A standard city bike is one hundred and twenty crowns for the day.'
questions.hint: Listen for the price of the standard bike, not the electric one.

questions.prompt: Why does the tourist choose the standard bike instead of the electric one?
options: Electric bikes are not available today
questions.explanation: He notes that he hasn't ridden much lately, but 'the city is quite flat.' The clerk confirms.
questions.hint: Listen for what they say about the city's terrain.

questions.prompt: What does the tourist give as ID?
questions.hint: Listen for the document he hands over.
Listen for the amount paid in advance.

questions.prompt: By what time must he return the bike to get the deposit back?
questions.explanation: 'You'll get it back when you return the bike before six o'clock.'

questions.prompt: What does the clerk say about helmets?
questions.explanation: 'helmets are free with every rental. It's not the law here, but we always recommend one.'
questions.hint: Listen for two facts about helmets together.

questions.prompt: What is in the small bag on the back of the bike?
questions.explanation: 'use the cable lock — it's in the small bag on the back.'
questions.hint: Listen for the second type of lock.

questions.prompt: What colour are the cycle paths the tourist should follow?
questions.hint: Listen for a colour at the end.

questions.prompt: How long will it take to reach the water?
questions.explanation: 'you'll be at the water in fifteen minutes.'
questions.hint: Listen for the time at the end of the conversation.

cloze.template:

Clerk: Of course. Just one day, or longer? We have hourly, daily, and weekly {{b2}}.
Tourist: Just today, please. I want to ride along the {{b3}} and maybe up to the little mermaid statue.
Clerk: That's a lovely route. A standard city bike is one hundred and twenty crowns for the day. An {{b4}} one is two hundred and fifty.
Tourist: I'll take the standard one. I haven't ridden much lately, but the city is quite {{b5}}, right?
Clerk: Very flat. You {{b6}} have any trouble. Could I see some ID, please? And we'll need a small deposit.
Tourist: Here's my passport. How much is the deposit?
Clerk: Two hundred crowns. You'll get it back when you return the bike before six o'clock.
Tourist: Got it. Do you provide a {{b7}}?
Clerk: Yes, helmets are free with every rental. It's not the law here, but we always recommend one.
Tourist: I'll definitely wear one. And what about a {{b8}}?
Clerk: Every bike comes with a wheel lock. If you stop somewhere busy, please also use the cable lock — it's in the small bag on the back.
Tourist: Sounds good. One last thing — do you have a {{b9}} with a quiet route to the harbour?
Clerk: I'll mark one for you. Stay on the green cycle {{b10}}, and you'll be at the water in fifteen minutes. Enjoy your ride!

blanks.explanation: 'rent' — to pay to use something for a short time.
'electric' — the more expensive option uses a battery.
'flat' — the city has no big hills.
'shouldn't' — 'You shouldn't have any trouble' reassures the tourist.
'lock' — needed to keep the bike safe.
'paths' — cycle paths are special lanes for bikes.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Copenhagen makes cycling very easy with flat streets and clear cycle paths. In your own city, what would have to change to make people choose a bike instead of a car? Whose decision is that — the cyclist, the planner, or both?
